items.headline: What just happened?! Explaining rare call that ended Pirates-Rockies thriller

news_items.headline: Manfred: Giants' Pride cap guidance was 'not clear...
news_items.summary: MLB commissioner Rob Manfred said in a letter to Missouri Sen. Josh Hawley that the Giants failed to properly explain to players that they were allowed to decline to wear rainbow-themed caps during the club's annual Pride Night earlier this month.

news_items.headline: Brewers' Woodruff gets 900th K in return from IL
news_items.summary: Brandon Woodruff recorded his 900th career strikeout and took a perfect game into the sixth inning in his first start for Milwaukee since April 30.

news_items.headline: Dodgers' Tucker exits game with low back spasms
news_items.summary: Dodgers outfielder Kyle Tucker was removed from Monday's game against the Twins in the second inning because of low back spasms. Manager Dave Roberts said he considered Tucker day-to-day.

news_items.headline: White Sox's Teel off IL, will make season debut
news_items.summary: The Chicago White Sox activated Kyle Teel from the 60-day injured list Monday, and hours later, the 24-yer-old catcher was in the lineup for his season debut, finishing 0-for-4 in his team's 6-5 victory over the Cleveland Guardians.

news_items.headline: Reds expect De La Cruz to return from IL Tuesday
news_items.summary: Reds shortstop Elly De La Cruz, who made three rehab starts for Triple-A Louisville, is expected to be reinstated from the injured list Tuesday after missing 19 games because of a strained right hamstring, Cincinnati manager Terry Francona said Monday.

news_items.headline: Mets' Lindor to play at least 1 more rehab game
news_items.summary: Francisco Lindor will play his third rehab game Tuesday for Triple-A Syracuse, at which point the Mets will assess whether to activate their shortstop from the injured list after a two-month absence because of a strained left calf.

news_items.headline: ⚾ Execs under most pressure at trade deadline
news_items.summary: With the deadline Aug. 3, here are which front offices could make -- or break -- their future this trade season.

news_items.headline: Braves offense lifeless again in loss to Padres
news_items.summary: The Braves offense was lifeless against Michael King and the San Diego bullpen on Monday night, losing 1-0 to drop their second game in a row. It was more of the same for Grant Holmes, who labored through 4 2/3 innings. He hung a slider to struggling Manny Machado in the fourth inning that was […]

news_items.headline: Bagpipes and baseball: Why was Scotland's Tartan Army cheering for the Miami Marlins?
news_items.summary: After celebrating with the Red Sox, members of Scotland's famed Tartan Army marched into loanDepot park and adopted the Marlins for a night.

news_items.headline: Tartan Army takes over Miami, turns Marlins game into 'electric' night
news_items.summary: The Tartan effect made its way to Miami - and provided one of the more unforgettable nights in the history of typically moribund loanDepot Park.

news_items.headline: Boomer Sooner! Oklahoma wins 1st MCWS title since '94
D-backs again can’t get big hit, fall to Cardinals
news_items.summary: Record: 39-39. Pace: 81-81. Change on 2025: -1. I suppose we should have predicted a one-run game here, and it may not be the last in the series. Coming in, the D-backs and Cardinals were first and equal second (with the Dodgers) for the most one run games in the National League. Arizona were 13-12, […]

news_items.headline: An Australian and his Foster: Nationals 4, Phillies 1
news_items.summary: The offense was absent in a rain-soaked loss

news_items.headline: Rockies 3, Red Sox 2: Giving up eight straight hits to lose it? That’s 2026 for ya
news_items.summary: LOL. LMAO even. The Sox never trailed until the final run crossed the plate, having built a 2-0 win methodically over the previous eight innings. This was a Little League-ass game (complimentary enough): Against starting pitchers neither team could do much, but the Sox eventually broke through on a sixth-inning RBI double by Willson Contreras. […]
